response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with genuine low-latency/high-throughput distributed-systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/second with async I/O, fibers, and caching) and authentic geospatial/GIS background, which maps well to the routing-pipeline domain. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and the candidate's C/C++ work is limited and dated (NI LabWindows/CVI, a bachelor-thesis audio project), with no Rust and recent experience centered on TypeScript/Node.js. They could plausibly grow into the stack given demonstrated breadth and willingness to learn, but they are not a direct match on the core language requirement.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput distributed-systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching, real-time metadata)
Authentic geospatial/GIS domain experience across multiple projects
Strong engineering rigor: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, and broad full-stack adaptability
concerns: C/C++ experience is limited and dated; recent commercial work is TypeScript/Node.js, not modern C++
No Rust experience to satisfy the acceptable-alternative requirement
GIS background is web-mapping focused, not production routing-tile pipelines
